content_preview.title: Pokémon Champions Stumbles Out of the Gate as Competitive Pokémon's New Mandatory Platform
content_preview.summary: Nintendo's dedicated competitive battler launched April 8 to mixed reviews, fan backlash over missing items and bugs, yet remains the official VGC platform for the 2026 season.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Pokémon Champions, the new dedicated competitive battling title from The Pokémon Company and developer The Pokémon Works, launched on April 8, 2026, for Nintendo Switch and Switch 2 — and immediately walked into a wall of criticism from the competitive community it was designed to serve. The game, which replaces the mainline Pokémon RPGs as the official platform for the Video Game Championship (VGC) circuit, [debuted to a Metacritic score of 67 out of 100](https://www.metacritic.com...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Re-review (Round 2). 931-word News article on Pokémon Champions launch. Both Round 1 findings addressed: Metacritic score now sourced directly, monetization prices now attributed to Nintendo Life. Article is well-structured with launch details, competitive transition context, community reaction, and a thoughtful analysis section.
editor_notes.source_verification: 6 sources read. (1) Pokemon.com (launch) ✅ — April 8 on Switch/Switch 2, The Pokémon Works, free-to-start, team building via recruit/HOME import, Ranked/Casual/tournament formats, Mega Meganium (Mega Sol)/Emboar (Mold Breaker)/Feraligatr (Dragonize), mobile 2026 with cross-platform, Indianapolis Regionals as first VGC event confirmed. (2) Kotaku ✅ — Life Orb/Choice Band/Choice Specs/Assault Vest missing confirmed, Mega Raichu X without Mega Stone confirmed, 3v3/4v4 (no 6v6) including private matches confirmed, player quote 'Genuinely, who was this game made for' confirmed. Fake Out bug implied in 'buggy move interactions.' (3) Pokemon.com (events) ✅ — Indianapolis May 29-31, NA Internationals June 12-14, World Championships August 28-30, mandatory September 1 2026 for CP events all confirmed. (4) Metacritic ✅ — Score of 67 confirmed, 22 critic reviews confirmed, 23% positive/77% mixed. (5) Nintendo Life ✅ — Starter Pack $6.99, Battle Pass $9.99/season, Membership $4.99/month or $49.99/year all confirmed verbatim. (6) GameSpot — Returns 403 to WebFetch; automated review confirmed reachable via Archive.org (original returned 200). GameSpot 7/10 score and Jake Dekker's quote are accepted per the source snapshot.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Both Round 1 findings properly resolved with correct attributions. Metacritic score and Nintendo Life pricing confirmed exact. No hallucinations.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. The analysis section presents the competing perspectives fairly — acknowledges design merits while being direct about community disappointment. No advocacy language.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald Pokémon Champions coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approve. Both Round 1 findings resolved with verified primary sources. All 6 sources confirmed accurate for their attributed claims.
